Build a production-grade backend system using .NET 8 (ASP.NET Core Web API) for an Intelligent Document Processing and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) platform.

The system should follow clean architecture principles and be modular, scalable, and extensible.

-----------------------------------
CORE REQUIREMENTS
-----------------------------------

1. Architecture:
- Use Clean Architecture with layers:
  - API (Controllers)
  - Application (Business Logic, Interfaces)
  - Domain (Entities)
  - Infrastructure (DB, external services)
  - Separate modules for OCR and RAG

2. Features:

A. Document Upload
- Endpoint: POST /api/documents/upload
- Accept PDF/image files
- Store file locally (initially, S3 later)
- Save metadata in PostgreSQL

B. Document Tracking
- Status field: Uploaded, Processing, Completed, Failed
- Endpoint to check status

C. OCR Processing (Stub initially)
- Create OCR service interface
- Mock implementation (real AWS Textract later)
- Extract text from documents

D. Processing Pipeline
- Split extracted text into chunks
- Prepare for embedding generation

E. RAG Pipeline (Basic version)
- Create interface for embedding service
- Store embeddings in PostgreSQL (pgvector compatible schema)
- Create endpoint:
  POST /api/rag/query

F. Query Flow:
- Accept user question
- Return mock response using placeholder logic
- Structure ready for real LLM integration

-----------------------------------
TECH STACK
-----------------------------------

- .NET 8 Web API
- Entity Framework Core
- PostgreSQL
- pgvector-compatible schema
- Dependency Injection
- Swagger enabled

-----------------------------------
DATABASE DESIGN
-----------------------------------

Tables:

1. Documents:
- Id (Guid)
- FileName
- StorageUrl
- Status
- CreatedAt

2. DocumentChunks:
- Id
- DocumentId
- Text
- Embedding (vector placeholder)

-----------------------------------
NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
-----------------------------------

- Use async/await everywhere
- Use interfaces for all services
- Follow SOLID principles
- Add logging
- Use DTOs (do not expose entities directly)
- Proper error handling and HTTP status codes

-----------------------------------
FOLDER STRUCTURE
-----------------------------------

src/
  Api/
  Application/
    Interfaces/
    Services/
    DTOs/
  Domain/
    Entities/
  Infrastructure/
    Data/
    Repositories/
  OCR/
  RAG/

-----------------------------------
DELIVERABLES
-----------------------------------

- Working .NET solution
- Controllers, services, interfaces
- DbContext + migrations
- Sample endpoints working via Swagger
- Mock OCR + mock RAG pipeline implemented

-----------------------------------
IMPORTANT
-----------------------------------

Do NOT integrate AWS Textract or OpenAI yet.
Keep those as interfaces and mock implementations.

Focus on:
- clean architecture
- extensibility
- production-ready code structure

Also generate:

1. Background processing design using queues (interface only)
2. Retry and failure handling strategy
3. Logging using built-in .NET logging
4. Separation of concerns between services
5. Dependency injection setup in Program.cs

Ensure the system is easily extendable for:
- AWS Textract integration
- OpenAI / Azure OpenAI RAG pipeline
- Vector database scaling
